page: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v5 is an EASv5-series virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs utilizing the x86_64 architecture at a clock speed of 3.525 GHz. The server is equipped with 672.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 14.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, though it is capable of utilizing Premium Storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core processing capabilities, with stress-ng multi-core, Geekbench multi-core, and PassMark CPU scores all placing in the top 25%. Memory subsystem performance is a key highlight, with the PassMark memory score reaching the top 10% tier, although uncached large-block memory read and write bandwidths remain in the middle 50% range. Single-core performance is average, as reflected in the single-threaded gzip compression and Geekbench single-core results.

The absence of local storage and GPU accelerators presents distinct tradeoffs. While the system achieves top-tier performance in text generation for small 135M parameter language models, text generation for large 70B models falls into the bottom 10% tier. The high memory density relative to compute resources makes this server class qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-bound workloads that do not require local NVMe drives. Recommended workloads include memory-intensive databases, large-scale caching layers, enterprise applications, and multi-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v5 is an EASv5-series virtual machine powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor. It features 48 vCPUs, 24 physical cores, and 672.0 GB of system memory, providing a high ratio of 14.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. Operating on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this server lacks local storage and GPU accelerators. Benchmarks show strong multi-core CPU performance and top-tier memory scores, though single-core execution and uncached memory bandwidth remain average. The system exhibits a clear tradeoff in machine learning, delivering top-tier text generation speeds for small language models but poor performance for large 70B models. This hardware profile is qualitatively cost-efficient for memory-intensive enterprise applications, large-scale caching, and database workloads that rely on network-attached Premium Storage rather than local NVMe drives.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine featuring 48 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 7763 processor and 672 GB of RAM. It delivers top-tier memory performance for database and memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E96-48as_v5 is an EASv5 virtual machine with 48 vCPUs, an AMD EPYC 7763 processor, and 672 GB of RAM, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated AMD EPYC processors and high RAM density for enterprise databases and memory-bound applications.
bullet_points: Dedicated AMD EPYC 7763 processor with 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores
672.0 GB of system memory providing a high 14.0 GB per vCPU ratio
Top-tier memory performance and strong multi-core processing benchmarks
No local storage or GPU accelerators, requiring network-attached storage
Fits memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
